info.title: Dexploit Streaming API
info.description: Real-time WebSocket streams: raw swaps and swap-event OHLCV candles. Authenticate with `?api_key=…` or `Authorization: Bearer …`.

production.description: Production WebSocket edge. TLS required. Authenticate with `?api_key=ohlcv_live_sk_…` or `Authorization: Bearer …`.

swaps.title: Raw swap stream
swaps.description: Every swap on every indexed Solana DEX, optionally filtered by pool, token, trader, dex, SOL amount, or direction. Send a `subscribe` message after connecting to set filters — by default, every swap flows through.

ohlcv.title: OHLCV candle stream
ohlcv.description: Live OHLCV bars for one or more pools / timeframes. Bars are emitted as they're built — `is_closed: false` mid-bar, `is_closed: true` on the final tick.

receiveSwapsSwap.description: One executed swap on a Solana DEX, normalized across protocols.

receiveSwapsPoolEvent.description: Non-swap pool activity — `lp_deposit`, `lp_withdraw`, `token_burn`, and the server-classified `rug_detected`. Delivered on an unfiltered or `tokens`/`pools`-filtered connection, interleaved with swaps. Demux on `event_type`. Only the `tokens`/`pools` filters apply to pool events; `dexes`/`min_sol`/`max_sol`/`traders`/`is_buy`/`wallet_tags` are swap-only. NOTE: `timestamp_ms` is epoch **milliseconds** (the `swap` frame uses `timestamp` in **seconds**); `quote_amount`/`lp_sol_pulled` are **lamports** despite no `_sol` suffix; `base_amount`/`amount_atomic` are atomic units; Meteora DAMM v2 (`dex=7`) saturates `lp_token_amount` to `u64::MAX`.

receiveSwapsRoomMessage.description: In room-mode, a swap OR pool event scoped to a joined room. The event payload is nested under `data` and carries `event_type` but NOT a top-level `type` — the **dual-frame trap** (same event, two on-wire shapes by mode). `transaction:{mint}` rooms interleave swaps + pool events + `rug_detected`.

receiveSwapsJoined.description: Sent after a successful `join`. The first `join` locks the connection into room-mode (mode-exclusive with filter-mode).

receiveSwapsSubscribed.description: Sent on connect AND after each successful `subscribe`. The connect-time frame includes a `client-N` ID for log correlation.

receiveSwapsPong.description: Reply to a client `ping`. Send a `ping` every ~25–30s to keep the connection from idling out.

receiveSwapsError.description: Validation failure, auth issue, or server-side error. Connection usually stays open after a recoverable error.

sendSwapsSubscribe.description: Replaces the active filter set. Empty `filters` means "every swap on every pool".

sendSwapsPing.description: Optional keepalive. Server replies with `pong`. Recommended every 25–30 seconds.

sendSwapsJoin.description: Subscribe to a room. The first `join` locks the connection into room-mode — mode-exclusive with `subscribe`/filter-mode. Valid rooms: `latest`, `graduating`, `graduated`, `rugs` (global rug feed — full RugRecord incl. `reason`+`graduated`), `safety:{mint}`, `price:{addr}:{tf}` (addr = mint OR pool; tf ∈ 1s/30s/1m/5m/15m/1h/4h/1d), `wallet:{wallet}`, `transaction:{mint}`, `transaction:{mint}:{pool}`, and the Pro+ PnL rooms `pnl:{wallet}`, `pnl:{wallet}:{token}`, `pnl:{wallet}:summary`. `transaction:{mint}` rooms interleave swaps + pool events + `rug_detected`. Per-tier join caps: Free 5, Developer 25, Pro 100, Enterprise unlimited.

receiveOhlcvOhlcv.description: One bar update for a subscribed (pool, timeframe). NOTE: `volume_sol` is in **lamports** despite the name (divide by 1e9 for SOL); `volume_token` is in base units (divide by 10**quote_decimals).

receiveOhlcvConnected.description: Sent once on connect with a UUID `client_id` and the index inception timestamp.

receiveOhlcvSubscribed.description: Sent after each successful op. `status`/`op` echo the op; `pairs` is the live distinct pool count and `subjects` the live (pool x timeframe) count AFTER the op. A subject cap rejection instead returns an error frame `{"error":"subject cap reached for this connection; cap <N>","cap":<N>}` and leaves the prior subscription intact.

receiveOhlcvError.description: Validation failure, auth issue, or server-side error. Connection usually stays open after a recoverable error.

sendOhlcvSubscribe.summary: Subscribe / add / remove / unsubscribe (pools x timeframes)
sendOhlcvSubscribe.description: First frame subscribes (arrays required). Later frames carry `op`: `add` unions symbols into the live set, `remove` drops them, `unsubscribe` clears all, `subscribe` (or op-absent) REPLACES the whole set. Mid-stream errors are non-fatal (socket stays open). Two caps: MAX_PAIRS_PER_SUBSCRIBE (per frame, default 1000) and MAX_SUBJECTS_PER_CONNECTION (cumulative live set, default 5000); an add/replace over the subject cap is rejected atomically.

ApiKeyQuery.name: api_key
ApiKeyQuery.description: `?api_key=ohlcv_live_sk_…` appended to the connection URL. Browser-friendly; the only option in environments where you can't set headers.

SwapsSubscribed.name: subscribed
SwapsSubscribed.title: Connection / subscribe ack
SwapsSubscribed.summary: Sent on connect AND after each successful `subscribe`. The connect-time frame includes a `client-N` ID for log correlation.

SwapEvent.name: swap
SwapEvent.title: Swap event
SwapEvent.summary: One executed swap on a Solana DEX, normalized across protocols.

PoolEvent.name: pool_event
PoolEvent.title: Pool event
PoolEvent.summary: Non-swap pool activity (filter-mode): LP deposit / withdraw, token burn, or the server-classified rug signal. All four share `type: "pool_event"` — demux on `event_type`. `lp_withdraw` is the real-time LP-removal signal; `rug_detected` is the classified version.

SwapsRoomMessage.name: message
SwapsRoomMessage.title: Room message
SwapsRoomMessage.summary: Room-mode delivery wrapper. The swap or pool event is nested under `data`; `data` carries `event_type` but NOT a top-level `type` (dual-frame trap).

SwapsJoin.name: join
SwapsJoin.title: Join room
SwapsJoin.summary: Subscribe to a room (e.g. `transaction:<mint>` or `transaction:<mint>:<pool>`). First `join` locks room-mode.

Pong.name: pong
Pong.title: Pong
Pong.summary: Reply to a client `ping`. Send a `ping` every ~25–30s to keep the connection from idling out.

StreamError.name: error
StreamError.title: Stream error
StreamError.summary: Validation failure, auth issue, or server-side error. Connection usually stays open after a recoverable error.

SwapsSubscribe.name: subscribe
SwapsSubscribe.title: Set filters
SwapsSubscribe.summary: Replaces the active filter set. Empty `filters` means "every swap on every pool".

Ping.name: ping
Ping.title: Heartbeat
Ping.summary: Optional keepalive. Server replies with `pong`. Recommended every 25–30 seconds.

OhlcvConnected.name: connected
OhlcvConnected.title: Connect hello
OhlcvConnected.summary: Sent once on connect with a UUID `client_id` and the index inception timestamp.

OhlcvSubscribed.name: subscribed
OhlcvSubscribed.title: Subscribe / add / remove ack
OhlcvSubscribed.summary: Sent after each successful op. `status` and `op` echo the op (`subscribed`/`added`/`removed`/`unsubscribed`). `pairs`/`subjects` are the new LIVE TOTALS after the op (not a delta). A subject = one (pool, timeframe) pair.

OhlcvFrame.name: ohlcv
OhlcvFrame.title: Candle update
OhlcvFrame.summary: One bar update for a subscribed (pool, timeframe). NOTE: `volume_sol` is in **lamports** despite the name (divide by 1e9 for SOL); `volume_token` is in base units (divide by 10**quote_decimals).

OhlcvSubscribe.name: subscribe
OhlcvSubscribe.title: Subscribe / add / remove / unsubscribe
OhlcvSubscribe.summary: Op-tagged frame that mutates the live (pool x timeframe) set. The FIRST frame subscribes; later frames on the same socket can `add`/`remove`/`unsubscribe` or `subscribe` (reset) without reconnecting. `op` absent = `subscribe` = REPLACE.

Dex.description: Lowercase snake_case DEX name. Note: on the WebSocket feed `dex` is the string name, but on the REST `/swaps*` endpoints it's an integer ID — these surfaces are intentionally different.

message.example: Connected as client-196. Send {"type":"subscribe","filters":{...}} to set filters.

error.description: Some errors return just `{"error":"…"}` instead of `{"type":"error","message":"…"}` — handle both.

cap.description: Present only on the per-connection subject-cap rejection: `{"error":"subject cap reached for this connection; cap <N>","cap":<N>}`. Non-fatal — the prior subscription stays intact.

filters.description: All filters combine as AND. Set to `null` or omit to disable a filter.

pools.description: Pool / pair address — usually the most useful filter.

tokens.description: Token mints. Matches any pool that trades this token.

is_buy.description: `true` for buys only, `false` for sells only.

WsSwapFrame.description: Streaming swap shape. Differs from the REST `Swap` schema — see the *Wire format gotchas* section in the WebSocket page.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch **seconds** (REST candles use ISO; REST swaps use ms — yes, all three are different).

swap_type.description: **Different from REST**: streaming uses `swap_type` (string), REST uses `is_buy` (bool).

amount_in.description: Base-unit amount of whichever side the trader sold. For buys, this is the input SOL.

virtual_sol_reserves.description: Pump.fun bonding curve only — null elsewhere.

lp_fee_bps.description: LP fee on this swap, in basis points.

fee.description: Aggregate lamports paid in fees; null when the protocol breaks it down per fee_bps field instead.

transfer_fee_in.description: Token-2022 transfer fee paid on the input side.

maker_tags.description: Wallet-intel tags applied to the maker (e.g. `sniper`, `insider`).

DexId.description: Integer (Enum8) DEX id used on **pool-event** frames — distinct from the string `Dex` used on swap frames. `2`=PumpSwap, `3`=RaydiumAmm, `4`=RaydiumClmm, `5`=RaydiumCpmm, `6`=Orca, `7`=MeteoraDammV2, `9`=MeteoraDlmm emit LP/rug pool events. `1`=PumpFun and `8`=MeteoraDbc are bonding curves (no fungible LP); `10`=MeteoraPools LP events are not yet emitted (shared dynamic vaults).

WsLpEventFrame.description: `lp_deposit` / `lp_withdraw` liquidity event. No buy/sell side and no price field.

timestamp_ms.description: Epoch **milliseconds** — the `swap` frame uses `timestamp` in epoch **seconds** (1000×).

base_amount.description: Memecoin side, **atomic** units. Divide by `10**decimals` (~6 for pump). Decimals are NOT carried on the event.

quote_amount.description: SOL / WSOL side in **lamports** despite no `_sol` suffix. Divide by `1e9` for SOL.

lp_token_amount.description: LP tokens, atomic. Meteora DAMM v2 (`dex=7`) **saturates this to `u64::MAX`** — use `base_amount`+`quote_amount` for value.

drained_pct.description: Fraction of pool liquidity removed by this event, 0..1. Constant-product pools use the LP-supply ratio; Meteora CL uses the SOL-reserve ratio. `0.0` when not computable.

pool_base_reserve_after.description: Post-event pool reserve, base side (atomic). `0` when N/A.

pool_quote_reserve_after.description: Post-event pool reserve, quote side (**lamports**). `0` when N/A.

lp_supply_after.description: Post-event total LP supply. Constant-product only — `0` for Meteora.

WsBurnRecordFrame.description: `token_burn` — a DIFFERENT shape: no `pool_address`, no base/quote.

WsRugRecordFrame.description: `rug_detected` — server-side classified rug / drain signal derived from an `lp_withdraw` or `lp_burn`. Fires on ANY non-`pumpfun` venue when a real LP drain crosses the drain threshold OUTSIDE the graduation/migration window (a genuine AMM/CLMM pool, not the pump.fun bonding curve, which has no fungible LP). Pump graduation-migrations are EXCLUDED.

signature.description: Base58 signature of the triggering withdraw/burn.

lp_sol_pulled.description: SOL liquidity removed, in **lamports**. Divide by `1e9` for SOL.

graduated.description: Whether the mint had already graduated/migrated off the pump.fun bonding curve when the drain landed. The graduation/migration window itself is excluded, so a `rug_detected` is always a post-migration (or never-pumped) AMM/CLMM drain.

WsPoolEventFrame.description: A pool-event frame (filter-mode). One of an LP deposit/withdraw, a token burn, or a classified rug. Demux on `event_type`.

WsRoomMessageFrame.description: Room-mode delivery wrapper. The swap or pool event is nested under `data`; `data` carries `event_type` but NOT a top-level `type` (the dual-frame trap — same event, two shapes by mode).

room.description: The room this message belongs to. One of the discovery rooms (`latest`/`graduating`/`graduated`/`rugs`), `safety:{mint}`, `price:{addr}:{tf}`, `wallet:{wallet}`, `transaction:{mint}`(`:{pool}`), or a `pnl:{wallet}`(`:{token}`|`:summary`) room. For the global `rugs` room, `data` is the full RugRecord (incl. `reason`+`graduated`, which REST `GET /rugs` omits).

data.description: The swap or pool event. For pool events, carries `event_type` (`lp_deposit`/`lp_withdraw`/`token_burn`/`rug_detected`) and the same fields as the filter-mode frame, minus the top-level `type`.

room.description: Room to join. Valid rooms: `latest`, `graduating`, `graduated`, `rugs`, `safety:{mint}`, `price:{addr}:{tf}`, `wallet:{wallet}`, `transaction:{mint}`, `transaction:{mint}:{pool}`, `pnl:{wallet}`, `pnl:{wallet}:{token}`, `pnl:{wallet}:summary` (the `pnl:*` rooms require Pro+). The first `join` locks room-mode (mode-exclusive with `subscribe`/filter-mode).

op.description: Mutation against the live (pool x timeframe) set. Absent = `subscribe`. `subscribe` REPLACES the whole set (reset); `add` unions; `remove` drops; `unsubscribe` clears all. The server reads `op` only — a legacy `type` field is ignored.

type.description: Legacy field, IGNORED by the server (it reads `op`). Retained for backward compatibility with the original one-shot subscribe frame.

pair_addresses.description: Pool / pair addresses. Required (>=1) for `subscribe`/`add`/`remove`; optional and ignored for `unsubscribe`.

timeframes.description: Required (>=1) for `subscribe`/`add`/`remove`; optional and ignored for `unsubscribe`.

status.description: Echoes the op that was applied. `op` field carries the same value.

op.description: Same value as `status`; the op-tagged ack form.

pairs.description: Distinct pool count in the live set AFTER this op (a total, not a delta). `0` after `unsubscribe`.

timeframes.description: Distinct timeframes in the live set after this op.

subjects.description: Total live (pool x timeframe) subjects after this op. `0` after `unsubscribe`.

timestamp.description: Bar open time. ISO 8601 — different from the `/ws/swaps` epoch-seconds convention.

is_closed.description: `false` for mid-bar updates, `true` on the final tick for that bar. A `(pair, timeframe, timestamp)` triple emitted with `is_closed: true` is the authoritative bar — earlier `is_closed: false` frames for that same triple are interim.
